channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UNDP Malawi Consultancy 2026: Digital Literacy Training Content Development Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Malawi has announced a consultancy opportunity for Digital Literacy Training Content Development under reference number UNDP-MWI-00935. This consultancy seeks a qualified national individual consultant to support the development of digital literacy training materials aimed at strengthening digital skills and inclusion initiatives in Malawi. The opportunity provides professionals in digital […]

The post UNDP Malawi Consultancy 2026: Digital Literacy Training Content Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Malawi has announced a consultancy opportunity for Digital Literacy Training Content Development under reference number UNDP-MWI-00935.

This consultancy seeks a qualified national individual consultant to support the development of digital literacy training materials aimed at strengthening digital skills and inclusion initiatives in Malawi. The opportunity provides professionals in digital education, instructional design, ICT training, and curriculum development with a chance to contribute to an impactful national development initiative supported by UNDP.

The consultancy will be implemented over a period of 30 working days spread across 7 months and will be managed through the UNDP Quantum Supplier Portal system.

About the Consultancy Opportunity

Digital literacy has become a critical component of social and economic development in today’s rapidly evolving digital world. Governments, international organizations, and development partners are increasingly investing in digital inclusion programmes to equip communities with essential digital competencies.

Through this consultancy, UNDP Malawi aims to develop effective, accessible, and high-quality digital literacy training content that can support learning initiatives, capacity-building programmes, and digital empowerment efforts across the country.

The selected consultant will play an important role in designing and developing educational materials that align with national development priorities and digital transformation goals.

Consultancy Details

Opportunity Title

Consultancy Services for Digital Literacy Training Content Development

Procurement Process

  • IC – Individual Contractor

Office

  • UNDP Malawi (UNDP-MWI)

Reference Number

  • UNDP-MWI-00935

Publication Date

  • 11 May 2026

Deadline

  • 25 May 2026 at 03:00 AM (New York Time)

Assignment Duration

  • 30 working days spread across 7 months

Purpose of the Consultancy

The consultancy focuses on creating digital literacy training content that can support educational and capacity-building programmes.

The assignment may involve:

  • Designing digital literacy curricula
  • Developing learning modules
  • Creating user-friendly educational materials
  • Structuring training manuals and guides
  • Supporting digital inclusion initiatives
  • Developing content for different learner levels
  • Ensuring accessibility and usability of training materials

The consultant is expected to deliver high-quality content that can effectively improve digital competencies among target beneficiaries.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Digital literacy is increasingly essential for:

  • Employment opportunities
  • Education access
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Civic participation
  • Access to digital services
  • Economic empowerment

This consultancy contributes directly to strengthening digital inclusion and empowering communities through technology and education.

Professionals selected for this role will contribute to meaningful development work while collaborating with one of the world’s leading international development organizations.

Who Should Apply?

This consultancy is ideal for professionals with expertise in:

  • Digital education
  • ICT training
  • Curriculum development
  • Educational content creation
  • Instructional design
  • E-learning development
  • Capacity-building programmes

Applicants with prior experience working with:

  • International organizations
  • NGOs
  • Government digital initiatives
  • Educational institutions
  • Community training programmes

may have a strong advantage.

Required Skills and Competencies

Although detailed terms of reference may be included in the official procurement documents, successful candidates are generally expected to demonstrate:

  • Strong writing and content development skills
  • Knowledge of digital literacy frameworks
  • Ability to design engaging learning materials
  • Understanding of adult learning methodologies
  • Experience in instructional design
  • Strong communication and organizational skills
  • Familiarity with digital inclusion programmes

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted directly through the UNDP Quantum Supplier Portal.

Interested applicants must:

  1. Register on the UNDP Quantum Supplier Portal
  2. Access the negotiation using the reference number UNDP-MWI-00935
  3. Submit proposals through the portal system

Applicants who do not yet have a supplier profile are required to complete supplier registration before submitting their application.

Clarification Requests

Any requests for clarification must be submitted in writing through the portal messaging functionality.

UNDP will respond in writing while maintaining confidentiality regarding the source of inquiries.

Important Application Information

Submission Deadline

Applications must be submitted before:

25 May 2026 at 03:00 AM (New York Time)

Late submissions may not be considered.

Contact Information

For procurement-related inquiries:

procurement.malawi@undp.org

Why Work with UNDP?

The United Nations Development Programme is one of the world’s leading development organizations, working across multiple sectors including:

  • Governance
  • Digital transformation
  • Poverty reduction
  • Education
  • Innovation
  • Sustainable development

Working with UNDP provides professionals with:

  • International development experience
  • Exposure to impactful projects
  • Opportunities for professional growth
  • Networking with global experts
  • Meaningful contribution to development goals

This consultancy offers an excellent opportunity for professionals passionate about digital empowerment and educational innovation to contribute to Malawi’s digital transformation efforts.

Here are optimized social media posts for different platforms.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post UNDP Malawi Consultancy 2026: Digital Literacy Training Content Development Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FCDO HELP-S Sudan Grant 2026: £30 Million Funding Opportunity for Humanitarian and Resilience Programmes
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) has launched the Humanitarian Emergency and Longer-term Planning – Sudan (HELP-S) programme, a major £30 million funding initiative aimed at strengthening resilience and humanitarian response systems in conflict-affected areas of Sudan. The programme will run for 2.5 years from 2026 to 2029 and seeks to support communities in […]

The post FCDO HELP-S Sudan Grant 2026: £30 Million Funding Opportunity for Humanitarian and Resilience Programmes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) has launched the Humanitarian Emergency and Longer-term Planning – Sudan (HELP-S) programme, a major £30 million funding initiative aimed at strengthening resilience and humanitarian response systems in conflict-affected areas of Sudan.

The programme will run for 2.5 years from 2026 to 2029 and seeks to support communities in more stable regions affected by the ongoing conflict in Sudan. Through this initiative, the FCDO aims to strengthen locally led humanitarian action, improve community resilience, and build sustainable response systems that can better address both immediate humanitarian needs and long-term recovery challenges.

This funding opportunity is particularly important for humanitarian organisations, local Sudanese actors, NGOs, academic institutions, and international agencies working to support vulnerable populations across Sudan.

About the HELP-S Programme

The Humanitarian Emergency and Longer-term Planning – Sudan (HELP-S) programme was developed in response to the growing humanitarian crisis and displacement challenges affecting Sudan.

The initiative recognizes that local communities are often the first responders during emergencies and that sustainable humanitarian responses require stronger partnerships between local and international actors.

The programme focuses on:

  • Supporting community resilience
  • Strengthening local humanitarian responders
  • Improving humanitarian coordination
  • Enhancing locally led approaches
  • Filling critical response gaps in stable but high-need areas
  • Promoting adaptive and flexible humanitarian programming

The FCDO aims to create a model that combines immediate humanitarian assistance with longer-term resilience planning to help communities better withstand future crises.

Funding Details

Total Funding Value

The HELP-S programme provides:

  • £30 million in total funding

Programme Duration

The programme will run for:

  • 2.5 years (2026–2029)

Funding Focus Areas

The programme targets sectors including:

  • Girls and women
  • Health
  • Humanitarian emergencies and disasters
  • Livelihoods
  • Community resilience

Organisations applying for funding are expected to develop innovative and sustainable approaches that address humanitarian needs while strengthening long-term resilience.

What the Fund Will Achieve

According to the FCDO, the HELP-S initiative is designed to transform humanitarian response models in Sudan by placing local actors at the center of humanitarian action.

Locally Led Humanitarian Response

The programme emphasizes that communities are the first responders during crises. The initiative therefore prioritizes:

  • Community-led action
  • Local partnerships
  • Sudanese leadership
  • Demand-driven humanitarian responses

Strong Partnerships

The programme encourages genuine collaboration between:

  • International humanitarian actors
  • Sudanese civil society organisations
  • Community groups
  • Research institutions
  • Relief agencies

These partnerships should be based on mutual respect, shared accountability, and complementary expertise.

Flexible and Adaptive Programming

Given the rapidly changing situation in Sudan, organisations must demonstrate the ability to:

  • Adapt activities quickly
  • Respond to changing humanitarian conditions
  • Operate effectively in volatile environments
  • Maintain programme continuity despite conflict-related challenges

Innovation and Accountability

The FCDO also expects funded organisations to:

  • Promote innovation in humanitarian delivery
  • Strengthen accountability to affected populations
  • Improve community acceptance
  • Uphold humanitarian principles

Eligible Organisations

The HELP-S programme is open to a wide range of humanitarian and nonprofit organisations.

Eligible applicants include:

  • International NGOs
  • Sudanese national NGOs
  • Local civil society organisations
  • United Nations agencies
  • Humanitarian relief organisations
  • Nonprofit academic institutions
  • Research organisations
  • Consortia partnerships

For-profit organisations are not eligible to apply directly but may participate as downstream partners in consortium arrangements.

Requirements for Applicants

To qualify for the programme, organisations must demonstrate several important operational and institutional capacities.

Humanitarian Experience

Applicants must show:

  • Proven experience delivering humanitarian assistance in challenging contexts
  • Experience operating in conflict-affected areas

Sudan Operational Capacity

Organisations must demonstrate:

  • Ability to work in hard-to-reach regions of Sudan
  • Understanding of local humanitarian dynamics
  • Strong local partnerships where possible

Safeguarding Systems

Applicants are required to have:

  • Strong safeguarding mechanisms
  • Measures to prevent sexual exploitation, abuse, and harassment
  • Accountability and protection systems

Commitment to Localisation

The FCDO places significant importance on:

  • Locally led humanitarian approaches
  • Genuine partnership with Sudanese organisations
  • Shared leadership and participation

Financial and Compliance Capacity

Eligible organisations must have:

  • Strong financial management systems
  • Compliance systems aligned with FCDO requirements
  • Transparent operational procedures

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  • A 5-page Expression of Interest using the official template
  • A high-level budget estimate using the provided budget template

Submission Deadline

The deadline to submit expressions of interest is:

27 May 2026 at 11:59 PM East Africa Time (EAT)

Late applications will not be considered.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post FCDO HELP-S Sudan Grant 2026: £30 Million Funding Opportunity for Humanitarian and Resilience Programmes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Refugees International 2026 Summer Internship Program: Apply for Humanitarian and Refugee Advocacy Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Refugees International has officially announced applications for its 2026 Summer Internship Program, offering students and emerging professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in humanitarian advocacy, refugee protection, migration policy, and international affairs. The internship program is designed for passionate individuals who want to contribute to meaningful global impact while developing professional experience in one […]

The post Refugees International 2026 Summer Internship Program: Apply for Humanitarian and Refugee Advocacy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Refugees International has officially announced applications for its 2026 Summer Internship Program, offering students and emerging professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in humanitarian advocacy, refugee protection, migration policy, and international affairs.

The internship program is designed for passionate individuals who want to contribute to meaningful global impact while developing professional experience in one of the world’s most respected refugee advocacy organizations.

Through this program, interns will work closely with experts, researchers, policy advocates, and communications teams focused on addressing humanitarian crises and improving conditions for displaced communities worldwide.

About Refugees International

Refugees International is a globally recognized humanitarian advocacy organization dedicated to protecting displaced people and promoting lifesaving assistance for refugees, asylum seekers, and vulnerable populations.

The organization conducts field research, policy analysis, and strategic advocacy to influence governments, international agencies, and humanitarian institutions. Refugees International works to ensure that displaced communities receive protection, dignity, and long-term solutions.

Its work spans multiple regions and humanitarian crises across the globe, making it an excellent learning environment for aspiring professionals interested in humanitarian response, migration policy, and global advocacy.

2026 Summer Internship Areas

The 2026 Summer Internship Program includes several specialized focus areas that allow applicants to contribute according to their interests and academic background.

Strategic Outreach Internship

The Strategic Outreach internship focuses on advocacy campaigns, public engagement, and communications strategies.

Interns may support:

  • Outreach initiatives
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Public awareness projects
  • Strategic communication efforts

This role is ideal for students interested in communications, advocacy, nonprofit management, and public engagement.

U.S. Asylum System Documentation Internship

This internship focuses on documenting and analyzing issues related to the U.S. asylum system.

Interns may contribute to:

  • Policy research
  • Documentation projects
  • Human rights analysis
  • Immigration policy monitoring
  • Community-centered advocacy initiatives

This opportunity is particularly suitable for applicants interested in immigration policy, law, human rights, and migration studies.

Localization and Refugee Leadership Internship

This internship highlights community-led humanitarian approaches and refugee leadership initiatives.

Interns may assist with:

  • Refugee leadership programs
  • Localization initiatives
  • Community partnerships
  • Inclusive humanitarian policies
  • Research and advocacy projects

Candidates passionate about refugee empowerment and participatory humanitarian systems are highly encouraged to apply.

Horn of Africa Internship

The Horn of Africa internship focuses on humanitarian and displacement issues affecting the region.

Interns may work on:

  • Regional conflict analysis
  • Refugee protection issues
  • Humanitarian response research
  • Policy recommendations
  • Crisis monitoring

This role is especially beneficial for students interested in African studies, international development, humanitarian affairs, and regional policy research.

Benefits of the Refugees International Internship Program

Participating in the Refugees International Summer Internship Program offers numerous advantages for students and young professionals.

Practical Humanitarian Experience

Interns gain direct exposure to international humanitarian advocacy and refugee protection work.

Professional Networking

Participants can connect with humanitarian experts, policymakers, researchers, and advocacy professionals.

Career Development

The internship helps build experience in:

  • Research
  • Policy analysis
  • Communications
  • Advocacy
  • International affairs
  • Humanitarian response

Global Exposure

Interns learn about real-world refugee crises, migration systems, and international humanitarian policies.

Meaningful Impact

Participants contribute to projects that support displaced communities and influence humanitarian policies globally.

Who Should Apply?

This internship program is ideal for:

  • Students seeking international experience
  • Graduates pursuing humanitarian careers
  • Individuals passionate about refugee rights
  • Young professionals interested in policy and advocacy
  • Applicants looking to build careers in international organizations and nonprofits

Application Information

Applicants are encouraged to prepare:

  • A strong resume or CV
  • A compelling cover letter
  • Relevant academic or professional experiences
  • Demonstrated interest in humanitarian advocacy and refugee protection

Applicants should carefully review each internship description before applying to ensure alignment with their interests and qualifications.

Deadline

No official application deadline was specified in the announcement image. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before positions are filled.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Refugees International 2026 Summer Internship Program: Apply for Humanitarian and Refugee Advocacy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Accelerator Grant 2027 by OCRA: Full Application Guide, Deadlines, Eligibility, and Research Funding Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The AI Accelerator Grant under the Ovarian Cancer Research Alliance (OCRA) 2027 funding cycle is a highly competitive, globally open research funding opportunity designed to support innovative, data-driven, and artificial intelligence–powered approaches in ovarian cancer research. This grant aims to accelerate scientific discovery by integrating AI technologies into oncology research, improving early detection, treatment strategies, […]

The post AI Accelerator Grant 2027 by OCRA: Full Application Guide, Deadlines, Eligibility, and Research Funding Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AI Accelerator Grant under the Ovarian Cancer Research Alliance (OCRA) 2027 funding cycle is a highly competitive, globally open research funding opportunity designed to support innovative, data-driven, and artificial intelligence–powered approaches in ovarian cancer research. This grant aims to accelerate scientific discovery by integrating AI technologies into oncology research, improving early detection, treatment strategies, precision medicine, and overall patient outcomes.

Applications for the AI Accelerator Grant are submitted through the SmartSimple grant management system. Researchers worldwide are encouraged to participate, provided they meet institutional eligibility requirements.

This article provides a comprehensive breakdown of the grant cycle, deadlines, application process, eligibility criteria, review structure, and key opportunities available under OCRA’s AI Accelerator Grant program.

Overview of the AI Accelerator Grant Cycle (2027)

OCRA runs a structured annual funding cycle that includes a Letter of Intent (LOI) phase followed by a full proposal phase (by invitation only). The AI Accelerator Grant follows a clearly defined timeline:

Letter of Intent (LOI) Phase

  • LOI Opens: Friday, May 8, 2026
  • LOI Deadline: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET
  • Status: Current active cycle stage
  • Submission Platform: SmartSimple grant management system
  • Note: Late submissions or extensions are strictly not permitted

Applicants must first submit a Letter of Intent outlining their proposed research, objectives, methodology, and relevance to AI in ovarian cancer research. Only selected applicants will be invited to submit full proposals.

Informational Webinar

OCRA will host a live informational webinar for prospective applicants:

  • Date: Tuesday, May 19, 2026
  • Time: 4:00 PM ET
  • Purpose: To provide guidance on application requirements, eligibility, and evaluation criteria
  • Recording: Available later on OCRA’s official website for those unable to attend

This session is highly recommended for applicants seeking clarity on expectations and proposal alignment.

Full Proposal Stage (By Invitation Only)

After rigorous peer review of LOIs:

  • Full Proposal Invitation Notifications: August 2026
  • Full Proposal Deadline: Thursday, September 10, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET
  • Submission Requirement: Only invited applicants may proceed to this stage

Selected researchers will be asked to submit detailed proposals with expanded methodology, preliminary data, budget plans, and implementation strategies.

Funding Notification

  • Expected Funding Decisions: Mid-November 2026

Successful applicants will receive formal notification and funding allocation details following final review by the Scientific Advisory Committee.

Review and Evaluation Process

OCRA ensures a rigorous, transparent, and confidential peer-review system. All submitted proposals are evaluated by a Scientific Advisory Committee.

Key aspects of the review process include:

  • Scientific merit and innovation
  • Feasibility and methodology
  • Impact on ovarian cancer research
  • Integration of artificial intelligence tools
  • Strength of research design and data strategy

All review activities, including scoring, deliberations, and critiques, are strictly confidential. Reviewers are required to sign confidentiality and conflict-of-interest agreements.

Eligibility Criteria

The AI Accelerator Grant is open to a wide range of qualified research institutions globally. However, applicants must meet strict organizational requirements.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Accredited non-profit universities and colleges
  • Non-profit research hospitals
  • Recognized non-profit scientific research institutions

Ineligible entities:

  • For-profit companies
  • Private consulting firms
  • Non-profits not directly conducting research

Applicants must ensure that the host institution is responsible for the research execution.

Additional OCRA 2027 Grant Opportunities

Beyond the AI Accelerator Grant, OCRA offers several other funding mechanisms under its 2027 grant cycle, including:

  • Collaborative Research Development Grant
  • Collaborative Research Development Grant – AI
  • Early Career Investigator Grant
  • Mentored Investigator Grant
  • Health Equity Research Grant
  • Rivkin Pilot Grant

Each grant targets different stages of research development and investigator experience levels.

Application Platform

All applications must be submitted through the SmartSimple grant management system. Applicants are advised to register early, prepare documents in advance, and ensure compliance with formatting and submission guidelines.

Important Notes

  • Extensions will not be granted under any circumstances
  • Late submissions will not be accepted
  • Only invited applicants proceed to full proposal stage
  • LOIs are reviewed by expert scientific panels worldwide
  • International applicants are encouraged to apply

Why This Grant Matters

The AI Accelerator Grant represents a strategic investment in the future of oncology research. By integrating artificial intelligence into ovarian cancer studies, this funding opportunity supports breakthroughs in:

  • Early detection algorithms
  • Predictive modeling for patient outcomes
  • Drug discovery and treatment optimization
  • Personalized medicine approaches
  • Data-driven clinical decision-making

It is a critical opportunity for researchers working at the intersection of biomedical science and artificial intelligence.

Deadline

23rd June, 2026.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity,click here.

For more global opportunities,click here.

The post AI Accelerator Grant 2027 by OCRA: Full Application Guide, Deadlines, Eligibility, and Research Funding Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OECD Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation Job in France 2026 | Apply for an International Policy Career in Paris
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development has officially opened applications for the prestigious Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation position based in Paris, France. This is an exciting international career opportunity for young professionals and early-career policy experts who are passionate about governance, evaluation, international development, public policy, research, and institutional performance assessment. The OECD […]

The post OECD Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation Job in France 2026 | Apply for an International Policy Career in Paris appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development has officially opened applications for the prestigious Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation position based in Paris, France. This is an exciting international career opportunity for young professionals and early-career policy experts who are passionate about governance, evaluation, international development, public policy, research, and institutional performance assessment.

The OECD is one of the world’s leading international organisations, working with governments and institutions to design better policies for better lives. Through this opportunity, selected candidates will contribute directly to the OECD’s evaluation systems and policy implementation processes while working in a multicultural and highly professional international environment.

The Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation role is ideal for candidates with strong analytical, research, communication, and policy evaluation skills who are interested in influencing global public governance and institutional effectiveness.

According to the official announcement, applications must be submitted before midnight on May 24, 2026 (Paris time).

About the OECD

The OECD is an international organisation composed of member countries that work together to promote economic growth, public governance, trade, social development, sustainability, and global cooperation. Headquartered in Paris, France, the OECD provides governments with evidence-based analysis and policy recommendations across various sectors.

Working at the OECD provides professionals with a unique opportunity to contribute to global policymaking while collaborating with experts from different countries and disciplines.

About the Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation Position

The Evaluation Division (EVIA) at the OECD is seeking a Junior Policy Analyst to support the successful implementation of its in-depth evaluation programme. The selected candidate will contribute to assessing the impact, effectiveness, and relevance of OECD committees and policy programmes.

The role focuses heavily on policy evaluation methodologies, stakeholder engagement, evidence analysis, and institutional performance assessment.

The successful applicant will support evaluation processes by:

  1. Conducting policy and programme analysis.
  2. Assessing implementation outcomes and institutional effectiveness.
  3. Applying evaluation methodologies and analytical frameworks.
  4. Collecting and analysing internal and external data.
  5. Conducting interviews and surveys with stakeholders.
  6. Supporting OECD committee evaluations.
  7. Preparing reports, presentations, and policy documents.
  8. Organising and facilitating meetings and discussions.
  9. Managing communication and coordination with stakeholders.
  10. Supporting OECD evaluation-related digital platforms and systems.

Benefits of the OECD Junior Policy Analyst Job

Selected candidates will receive several professional and financial benefits, including:

  • A starting monthly salary from approximately EUR 4,638.
  • Additional allowances depending on eligibility.
  • Exposure to international policymaking processes.
  • Opportunity to work in Paris, France.
  • Career development within an internationally recognised institution.
  • Networking opportunities with global experts and policymakers.
  • Experience in policy evaluation and governance systems.
  • A multicultural and inclusive working environment.
  • Long-term career growth opportunities within international organisations.

Required Qualifications

Applicants are expected to possess strong academic and professional backgrounds relevant to policy analysis and evaluation.

Preferred qualifications include:

  • A university degree in public policy, economics, political science, international relations, social sciences, statistics, evaluation studies, or related fields.
  • Experience in research, policy analysis, evaluation, governance, or programme assessment.
  • Strong analytical and drafting skills.
  • Ability to work with qualitative and quantitative data.
  • Excellent communication and organisational skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in multicultural environments.
  • Fluency in English or French, with willingness to learn the second OECD official language.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity provides early-career professionals with the chance to gain hands-on experience in international governance and evaluation systems while contributing to global policy development.

Working with the OECD can significantly strengthen your international career profile, particularly if you aspire to work in:

  • International organisations
  • Government institutions
  • Public policy think tanks
  • Research institutes
  • Development organisations
  • Monitoring and evaluation sectors
  • Governance and public administration

The position also offers exposure to high-level policy discussions and international collaboration.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: May 24, 2026 (Midnight Paris Time).

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official OECD recruitment portal.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post OECD Junior Policy Analyst – Evaluation Job in France 2026 | Apply for an International Policy Career in Paris appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canada’s STEP Program 2026: Paid Green Jobs and Wage Subsidies for Youth and Employers in Natural Resources
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Young people in Canada looking to launch meaningful careers in sustainability, environmental innovation, mining, forestry, clean energy, and earth sciences now have a major opportunity through the Science and Technology Employment Programme (STEP). Formerly known as the Science and Technology Internship Program (STIP), STEP has officially rebranded for 2026 while continuing its mission of creating […]

The post Canada’s STEP Program 2026: Paid Green Jobs and Wage Subsidies for Youth and Employers in Natural Resources appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people in Canada looking to launch meaningful careers in sustainability, environmental innovation, mining, forestry, clean energy, and earth sciences now have a major opportunity through the Science and Technology Employment Programme (STEP). Formerly known as the Science and Technology Internship Program (STIP), STEP has officially rebranded for 2026 while continuing its mission of creating green jobs and supporting workforce development across Canada’s natural resources sector.

Supported by Natural Resources Canada and delivered through Canada Green Corps (CGC), the STEP Program connects youth aged 15–30 with paid, hands-on employment opportunities in industries that are shaping Canada’s sustainable future. At the same time, employers receive substantial wage subsidies to help them recruit and train emerging talent.

The program is designed to benefit both job seekers and employers by creating practical work experiences, building industry skills, and supporting environmental and economic development across Canada.

What is the STEP Program?

The Science and Technology Employment Programme (STEP) is a Canadian employment initiative focused on helping youth gain practical work experience in natural resource sectors while helping employers reduce hiring costs through wage subsidies.

The initiative supports green employment opportunities connected to:

  • Clean energy
  • Forestry and conservation
  • Mining and minerals
  • Environmental sciences
  • Geomatics and earth observation
  • Climate change adaptation
  • Sustainable resource development
  • Environmental protection

As of April 2026, the program officially transitioned from STIP to STEP, but its core objective remains the same: preparing the next generation of professionals for Canada’s growing green economy.

Opportunities Available for Job Seekers

Young Canadians interested in environmental sustainability, science, technology, natural resources, engineering, or climate-focused careers can benefit significantly from STEP.

Participants gain:

  1. Paid full-time work experience
  2. Industry-relevant training
  3. Career mentorship
  4. Professional networking opportunities
  5. Exposure to Canada’s green economy
  6. Long-term employment prospects
  7. Practical field and office experience

The placements are intended to help youth become job-ready while contributing to environmentally responsible projects and initiatives.

The program specifically supports opportunities in sectors such as:

Energy Sector

  • Clean energy technologies
  • Smart grids and energy storage
  • Energy efficiency projects
  • Sustainable development of non-renewable resources

Forestry Sector

  • Forestry management
  • Conservation and ecology
  • Bioeconomy product manufacturing
  • Wood product manufacturing
  • Pulp and paper production

Mining, Minerals, and Metals

  • Exploration and prospecting
  • Extraction and milling
  • Manufacturing and processing
  • Mining support services
  • Critical minerals development
  • Mine reclamation and closure

Earth Sciences and Environmental Support

  • Environmental assessments
  • Climate change adaptation
  • Geomatics and mapping
  • Hazard prevention and analysis
  • Sustainable development projects

These placements help young professionals build practical experience in industries that are increasingly important to Canada’s economy and environmental future.

STEP Wage Subsidies for Employers

Employers across Canada can also benefit from STEP by accessing wage subsidies that significantly reduce hiring costs.

Organizations hiring eligible youth may receive:

  • Up to 75% wage subsidies for qualifying positions
  • Up to $20,000 for job-ready youth hires
  • Up to $24,000 for youth from employment equity groups
  • Up to $27,000 for youth experiencing barriers

Funding is available for employers operating in natural resource sectors and green economy industries. Priority is given to employers hiring youth from underrepresented and equity-deserving communities.

Employers Eligible to Apply

Eligible employers may include:

  • Private companies
  • Non-profit organizations
  • Indigenous organizations
  • Municipal and provincial entities
  • Environmental organizations
  • Mining and energy companies
  • Forestry enterprises
  • Sustainability-focused businesses

The program helps employers:

  • Reduce recruitment costs
  • Build future talent pipelines
  • Increase organizational capacity
  • Support sustainability initiatives
  • Access motivated young workers
  • Contribute to workforce diversity

Who Can Apply as a Job Seeker?

To qualify for STEP, applicants must:

  • Be between 15 and 30 years old at the start of placement
  • Be a Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or refugee
  • Be legally allowed to work in Canada
  • Work full-time (minimum 30 hours per week)
  • Be newly hired after April 1, 2026

The program does not support temporary summer jobs or short-term student placements. Participants are expected to engage in meaningful long-term employment opportunities.

Focus on Diversity and Inclusion

One of the major strengths of STEP is its commitment to inclusion and equitable employment opportunities.

The program prioritizes youth who may face barriers to employment, including:

  • Indigenous youth
  • Women in STEM and natural resources
  • Racialized youth
  • Youth with disabilities
  • Youth living in northern territories
  • Youth in remote communities

This focus helps create a more inclusive workforce while ensuring underrepresented communities can participate in Canada’s growing green economy.

Why the STEP Program Matters

Canada’s natural resources sector is undergoing significant transformation due to climate innovation, sustainability priorities, and clean technology development. Employers increasingly need skilled young professionals who understand environmental responsibility and emerging technologies.

STEP helps bridge the gap between education and employment by giving youth direct industry experience while helping employers strengthen their workforce.

The program also contributes to:

  • Environmental sustainability
  • Economic development
  • Workforce modernization
  • Climate action
  • Green innovation
  • Youth employment

For many participants, STEP becomes the foundation for long-term careers in environmental sciences, engineering, conservation, mining, energy, and sustainability-focused industries.

Application Deadline

There is currently no fixed deadline for STEP applications. However, funding is limited and applications are processed based on availability. Early application is strongly encouraged for both employers and job seekers.

Application Links

Job Seekers

Apply here: STEP Job Seekers Portal

Employers

Apply here: STEP Employers Portal

Official Program Information

Learn more: Canada Green Corps STEP Program

The STEP Program represents an outstanding opportunity for young Canadians to gain practical green economy experience while helping employers invest in the future workforce. Whether you are an aspiring environmental professional or an employer seeking skilled young talent, STEP offers valuable support to help build sustainable careers and industries across Canada.

Opportunity Category

Job / Employment Program

Eligible Applicants

  • Youth aged 15–30
  • Canadian citizens
  • Permanent residents
  • Refugees legally authorized to work in Canada
  • Employers in Canada’s natural resources sector

Eligible Countries/Regions

Canada

Eligible Continents

North America

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Canada’s STEP Program 2026: Paid Green Jobs and Wage Subsidies for Youth and Employers in Natural Resources appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google for Startups Immersion 2026 Powered by Antler: AI Founders Program for Scaling Startups Opens Applications
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Artificial intelligence startups around the world are rapidly transforming industries, and Google is once again stepping in to support the next generation of innovative founders through the Google for Startups Immersion Program 2026, powered by Antler. This highly competitive initiative is specifically designed for ambitious startup founders who are transitioning from a Minimum Viable Product […]

The post Google for Startups Immersion 2026 Powered by Antler: AI Founders Program for Scaling Startups Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence startups around the world are rapidly transforming industries, and Google is once again stepping in to support the next generation of innovative founders through the Google for Startups Immersion Program 2026, powered by Antler. This highly competitive initiative is specifically designed for ambitious startup founders who are transitioning from a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) to building scalable, defensible, AI-first companies.

The program combines Google’s advanced technical ecosystem with Antler’s deep operational and venture-building expertise to help founders refine their products, strengthen their technical architecture, and position their startups for long-term scale and investment readiness.

Applications are currently open, and interested founders must submit their applications before May 22, 2026.

What is the Google for Startups Immersion Program?

Google for Startups Immersion is a two-phase accelerator-style experience tailored for AI startup founders and technical leaders. The initiative is focused on helping startups move beyond experimentation and develop sustainable AI products capable of scaling globally.

Unlike many traditional startup accelerators that prioritize pitch competitions and investor showcases, this program emphasizes practical product development, technical optimization, architecture reviews, and hands-on support from Google engineers and leading AI experts.

The program is powered in collaboration with Antler, one of the world’s most active early-stage venture capital firms known for supporting high-potential founders and scalable technology ventures.

Participants will gain direct exposure to:

  • Google’s AI ecosystem and infrastructure
  • Technical guidance from Google engineers
  • Startup scaling insights from Antler
  • Advanced AI workflows and architecture
  • Real-world product optimization strategies
  • Investor and venture-building perspectives

This program is particularly valuable for startups building AI-first products and seeking technical depth rather than just business networking.

Program Structure

The Google for Startups Immersion Program 2026 consists of two carefully designed phases that combine online learning with in-person technical collaboration.

Phase One: Online Program

Dates:

June 2–11, 2026

The first phase is conducted virtually and includes four intensive sessions designed specifically for startup founders and Chief Technology Officers (CTOs).

The online sessions will cover advanced topics critical for scaling AI startups, including:

1. Agent Architecture

Participants will explore how to design scalable and efficient AI agents capable of handling complex workflows and real-world business operations.

2. Google Antigravity Workflows

Founders will learn advanced workflow strategies and AI optimization methods using Google’s ecosystem and infrastructure.

3. Venture Capital Scaling Mathematics

The program dives into the “VC math” behind startup growth, helping founders understand:

  • Scalability metrics
  • Product-market fit indicators
  • Growth efficiency
  • Capital utilization
  • Sustainable expansion strategies

4. Founder and CTO Dedicated Tracks

The program separates strategic business discussions from technical engineering tracks, ensuring that both founders and technical leaders receive tailored guidance relevant to their roles.

This first phase aims to help startups identify weaknesses, optimize technical systems, and prepare for scalable growth.

Phase Two: In-Person Residency at Google

Date:

June 26, 2026

Location:

Bengaluru, India

Following the online phase, the top 25 startups will be selected to participate in an exclusive, invite-only in-person residency hosted at Google’s offices in Bengaluru.

Unlike traditional startup demo days, this residency focuses heavily on technical collaboration and product refinement.

Activities during the residency include:

Live Architecture Reviews

Participants will receive direct technical feedback on their AI systems, infrastructure, workflows, and scalability strategies from experienced Google engineers.

Bug Fixing Sessions

Founders and CTOs will work alongside Google experts to identify and solve technical bottlenecks and improve system performance.

One-on-One Sessions

Selected startups will have personalized meetings with:

  • Antler venture experts
  • Google technical teams
  • Leading AI founders
  • Product scaling specialists

Real Product Development Support

The program emphasizes practical problem-solving rather than presentations and pitch decks.

As highlighted by the organizers:
“No pitch decks. Just live architecture reviews, bug fixing with Google engineers, and 1:1 sessions with Antler and leading AI founders.”

This approach makes the program highly attractive for serious builders focused on execution and technical excellence.

Why Founders Should Apply

The Google for Startups Immersion Program offers several major advantages for AI startup founders.

Access to Google Expertise

Participants gain direct access to some of the world’s leading AI engineers, infrastructure specialists, and product experts.

Exposure to Venture-Building Insights

Antler’s involvement provides practical guidance on scaling startup operations, fundraising readiness, and business growth.

Technical Product Optimization

Startups receive valuable technical reviews that can significantly improve scalability, reliability, and product performance.

High-Level Networking

Selected startups connect with:

  • Experienced founders
  • Venture capital experts
  • AI ecosystem leaders
  • Google engineering teams

Focus on Execution

The program is intentionally designed to prioritize product building and technical problem-solving rather than polished presentations.

Who Should Apply?

The program is ideal for:

  • AI startup founders
  • Technical co-founders
  • CTOs
  • Early-stage startups with MVPs
  • Teams building scalable AI products
  • Founders looking to optimize architecture and infrastructure

The initiative is especially beneficial for startups that already have a working product and are preparing for rapid growth and scaling.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Google for Startups Immersion Program 2026 close on May 22, 2026.

Interested founders are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the highly competitive selection process.

Application Link

Apply here:

Final Thoughts

As AI continues to reshape industries globally, programs like Google for Startups Immersion provide founders with rare opportunities to work directly with world-class engineers, venture experts, and technical leaders.

The program’s strong emphasis on practical execution, architecture reviews, scalability, and AI optimization makes it stand out from traditional startup accelerators.

For founders serious about building scalable AI-first companies, this initiative could become a major stepping stone toward long-term success.

Eligible Applicants

  • Startup founders
  • AI startup teams
  • CTOs
  • Technical co-founders
  • Early-stage AI companies with MVPs

Discover more global accelerator programs on OFY here

The post Google for Startups Immersion 2026 Powered by Antler: AI Founders Program for Scaling Startups Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: State Street Education Scholarship Fund 2026–2027 in Ireland | Up to €1,000 for Marginalised Students
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Open Doors Initiative (ODI), with generous support from State Street, has officially launched the ODI State Street Education Scholarship Fund for the 2026–2027 academic year. This important funding opportunity is designed to support students from marginalised and underrepresented communities in Ireland who face financial barriers to accessing higher education. The scholarship programme aims to […]

The post State Street Education Scholarship Fund 2026–2027 in Ireland | Up to €1,000 for Marginalised Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Open Doors Initiative (ODI), with generous support from State Street, has officially launched the ODI State Street Education Scholarship Fund for the 2026–2027 academic year. This important funding opportunity is designed to support students from marginalised and underrepresented communities in Ireland who face financial barriers to accessing higher education.

The scholarship programme aims to empower learners pursuing third-level education by easing the burden of tuition and registration fees. Eligible applicants can receive scholarships of up to €1,000 to support their educational journey.

Applications are now open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the deadline on 3 June 2026.

About the ODI State Street Education Scholarship Fund

The Open Doors Initiative is committed to creating equal access to education, employment, and training opportunities for people from diverse and disadvantaged backgrounds. Through this partnership with State Street, ODI seeks to ensure that students facing systemic and financial barriers can continue or begin their higher education studies successfully.

The scholarship specifically supports individuals who may otherwise struggle to afford third-level education due to economic hardship and social exclusion.

Funding awarded through this programme can only be used toward:

  • Tuition fees
  • Registration fees

Applicants should note that proof of payment to the educational institution will be required.

Scholarship Value

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Scholarships worth up to €1,000 each

The number of scholarships awarded may vary depending on the number and quality of applications received.

ODI has indicated that there is expected to be strong interest in this fund, and successful applicants will be informed as soon as possible after the review process.

Eligible Courses

Applicants may apply if they are:

  • Currently attending a third-level course and have completed at least one year
  • Accepted into a course for the upcoming 2026/2027 academic year
  • Planning to apply for a course

Eligible programmes must be:

  • Full-time or part-time
  • Delivered during the 2026–2027 academic period
  • At NFQ Level 6 or higher

Required Application Materials

Applicants will need to complete the official application form and provide the following information:

Personal Information

  • First name
  • Last name
  • Email address
  • Telephone number

Educational Information

  • Course name
  • Institution name
  • Current stage of study

Personal Statement (Maximum 500 Words)

Applicants must submit a detailed personal statement explaining:

  • Their background
  • Academic goals
  • Career aspirations
  • Financial challenges
  • Why they are applying for the scholarship
  • The impact the scholarship would have on their future

The organisers note that applicants may use AI tools such as ChatGPT or Copilot to help structure or polish their responses. However, submissions that appear to be heavily AI-generated with minimal personal input may negatively affect the application.

Video Submission Requirement

In addition to the written application, applicants must submit:

  • A video presentation of up to 2 minutes

The video should explain:

  • Why the applicant would benefit from the scholarship
  • Their educational goals
  • Their personal story and motivation

Video Submission Instructions

Applicants must email their video to:

  • info@opendoorsinitiative.ie

The email subject line should include:

  • “State Street Educational Scholarship” followed by the applicant’s name

Important Conditions

Applicants should note the following conditions carefully:

  • The scholarship can only be used for tuition or registration fees
  • Proof of payment to the institution will be required
  • Applicants receiving or approved for a SUSI grant are not eligible
  • Applicants must complete the application themselves
  • Applicants must be participants of the Open Doors Initiative programme

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the ODI State Street Education Scholarship Fund is:

3 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to anticipated high demand.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more  scholarship opportunities here 

The post State Street Education Scholarship Fund 2026–2027 in Ireland | Up to €1,000 for Marginalised Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RICE360 Global Health Fellowship 2025: Fully Funded Engineering Fellowship for Emerging Innovators in Global Health Technologies
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Rice University through its RICE360 Institute for Global Health Technologies is inviting applications for the prestigious Global Health Fellowship, a one-year post-baccalaureate fellowship designed for exceptional early-career engineers passionate about creating life-saving medical technologies for underserved and low-resource communities around the world. This fellowship provides a rare opportunity for young innovators and engineering graduates […]

The post RICE360 Global Health Fellowship 2025: Fully Funded Engineering Fellowship for Emerging Innovators in Global Health Technologies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Rice University through its RICE360 Institute for Global Health Technologies is inviting applications for the prestigious Global Health Fellowship, a one-year post-baccalaureate fellowship designed for exceptional early-career engineers passionate about creating life-saving medical technologies for underserved and low-resource communities around the world.

This fellowship provides a rare opportunity for young innovators and engineering graduates to work directly on impactful global health projects while receiving mentorship from world-renowned experts in medical technology development. The program combines engineering innovation, humanitarian impact, hands-on research, and real-world healthcare problem-solving into one transformative experience.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis starting April 27.

About the Rice360 Global Health Fellowship

The Rice360 Global Health Fellowship is an intensive, hands-on program aimed at empowering the next generation of engineers and innovators to design affordable and high-performance healthcare technologies for low-resource settings.

The fellowship focuses on solving healthcare challenges that disproportionately affect vulnerable populations, especially in areas where access to advanced medical technology is limited.

Selected fellows will collaborate in multidisciplinary teams to:

  • Develop medical technologies
  • Refine healthcare devices
  • Evaluate product effectiveness
  • Conduct design and engineering research
  • Improve healthcare accessibility globally

The program emphasizes innovation that can directly improve survival rates and healthcare outcomes in underserved communities.

What Fellows Will Work On

Fellows will contribute to meaningful healthcare innovation projects. Previous projects have included:

  1. Warming cribs for newborn babies
    • Designed to help premature and vulnerable infants maintain safe body temperatures in low-resource hospitals.
  2. Diagnostic tests for jaundice
    • Affordable tools for early detection and treatment of neonatal jaundice.
  3. Machines for assisting newborn breathing
    • Low-cost respiratory support devices for newborns in healthcare facilities with limited resources.

These projects are specifically designed to address critical healthcare gaps in developing and underserved regions.

Work With Globally Recognized Innovators

One of the most valuable aspects of the fellowship is the opportunity to work closely with internationally recognized leaders in global health innovation, including:

  • Maria Oden
  • Rebecca Richards-Kortum

Their mentorship provides fellows with exposure to world-class engineering practices, humanitarian innovation strategies, and global healthcare systems.

Fellowship Benefits

The Rice360 Global Health Fellowship offers a comprehensive support package for selected fellows.

Fellows Will Receive:

  • Competitive salary
  • Full employee benefits package
  • Visa sponsorship for eligible international applicants
  • Funding for technology development
  • Potential international travel opportunities
  • Access to advanced research and development resources
  • Mentorship from leading innovators and engineers
  • Opportunity to work on impactful global health technologies

Additionally, fellows may have the opportunity to extend the fellowship up to two years based on performance and project needs.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Healthcare inequality remains a major challenge in many parts of the world. Millions of people lack access to reliable medical technologies due to cost, infrastructure limitations, and resource shortages.

The Rice360 fellowship addresses these challenges by empowering engineers to create practical, scalable, and affordable healthcare solutions that can save lives.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals who want to:

  • Combine engineering with humanitarian impact
  • Build a career in global health technologies
  • Gain hands-on medical device development experience
  • Work on real healthcare challenges
  • Develop leadership and innovation skills
  • Contribute to healthcare equity worldwide

Eligibility Requirements

The fellowship is open to:

  • Graduating seniors
  • Recent graduates
  • Individuals with a bachelor’s degree in:
    • Engineering
    • Physical sciences

Applicants must have graduated from an accredited college or university.

Preferred Candidates

The program highly encourages applications from individuals who have:

  • A master’s degree
  • Medical device industry experience
  • Biotechnology industry experience
  • Demonstrated interest in global health technology development

Applicants should clearly show their passion for healthcare innovation and problem-solving in low-resource environments.

Required Application Materials

Interested applicants must submit:

  1. A cover letter
  2. Resume/CV
  3. Academic transcript
  4. Three professional references

Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials that demonstrate technical ability, leadership potential, teamwork, and commitment to global health innovation.

Application Deadline

  • Applications are accepted on a rolling basis starting April 27.

Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible to improve their chances of selection.

How to Apply

Applicants can apply directly through the official Rice360 fellowship application portal.

Apply here

Official Website

Rice360 Global Health Fellowship

Contact Information

For additional information, applicants may contact:

  • rice360GHfellowship@rice.edu

About Rice360 Institute for Global Health Technologies

The RICE360 Institute for Global Health Technologies at Rice University is dedicated to developing innovative technologies that improve healthcare delivery globally, particularly in underserved communities.

The institute is known for its strong emphasis on:

  • Humanitarian engineering
  • Medical innovation
  • Global collaboration
  • Student-led research
  • Affordable healthcare technologies

Its programs have impacted healthcare systems and patient outcomes in multiple countries worldwide.

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here

The post RICE360 Global Health Fellowship 2025: Fully Funded Engineering Fellowship for Emerging Innovators in Global Health Technologies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: U.S. Mission to NATO Grants Program 2025: Funding Opportunities to Advance Transatlantic Defense, Security, and Industrial Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The U.S. Mission to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (USNATO) has announced funding opportunities under its Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program, designed to strengthen transatlantic defense cooperation, support NATO priorities, and reinforce U.S. leadership within the Alliance. Organizations, institutions, researchers, policy experts, civil society actors, and industry stakeholders working in areas related to defense, security, […]

The post U.S. Mission to NATO Grants Program 2025: Funding Opportunities to Advance Transatlantic Defense, Security, and Industrial Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Mission to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (USNATO) has announced funding opportunities under its Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program, designed to strengthen transatlantic defense cooperation, support NATO priorities, and reinforce U.S. leadership within the Alliance. Organizations, institutions, researchers, policy experts, civil society actors, and industry stakeholders working in areas related to defense, security, and industrial collaboration are encouraged to apply.

Located at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium, the U.S. Mission to NATO serves as the official representation of the United States to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization. Through diplomatic engagement, policy coordination, public diplomacy, and strategic partnerships, the mission works to advance peace, collective defense, and security cooperation among NATO member states and partner countries.

The grants program reflects the United States’ commitment to building a stronger, safer, and more prosperous Alliance by supporting innovative initiatives that contribute to NATO’s collective defense and industrial capacity.

About the U.S. Mission to NATO

The U.S. Mission to NATO represents U.S. foreign policy interests within the Alliance and plays a central role in diplomatic coordination among NATO member countries.

The Mission supports engagement at the highest political and military levels through the North Atlantic Council (NAC), NATO’s principal political decision-making body. The NAC brings together representatives from all NATO member states to discuss and coordinate responses to political, defense, and operational security matters affecting the Alliance.

The U.S. Permanent Representative to NATO occupies the U.S. chair in the NAC and oversees diplomatic representation within NATO structures.

Key Officers at the U.S. Mission to NATO

The leadership team at the U.S. Mission to NATO includes:

  • Matthew Whitaker – Permanent Representative of the United States to NATO
  • Scott Oudkirk – Deputy Chief of Mission
  • Scott Delius – Senior Civilian Representative of the Secretary of War in Europe and Defense Advisor
  • Stephen Gee – Political Advisor
  • Aaron Honn – Public Affairs Advisor

These officials help coordinate U.S. engagement on defense, diplomacy, strategic cooperation, and public affairs initiatives across the NATO Alliance.

Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program

The Office of the Public Affairs Advisor manages the Public Diplomacy Small Grants Program. The initiative supports projects and partnerships that advance key U.S. and NATO priorities across:

  • Academia
  • Public policy
  • Defense and security sectors
  • Industry collaboration
  • Civil society organizations
  • Strategic research institutions

The grants program prioritizes projects that:

  1. Reinforce U.S. leadership within NATO
  2. Advance defense investment and industrial capacity
  3. Strengthen transatlantic security cooperation
  4. Build professional expertise and strategic networks
  5. Translate NATO commitments into practical outcomes
  6. Support a stronger and more resilient Alliance

The program is designed to encourage innovative ideas and collaborative solutions that improve collective defense readiness and deepen international cooperation among NATO member states and partner countries.

Funding Opportunity: Advancing NATO’s Collective Defense and Industrial Capacity

One of the highlighted funding opportunities currently available through the U.S. Mission to NATO is:

Advancing NATO’s Collective Defense and Industrial Capacity

  • Funding Opportunity Number: OFOP0002706

This funding opportunity focuses on initiatives that contribute to NATO’s operational readiness, defense infrastructure, industrial resilience, and long-term security cooperation among allied nations.

Applicants are encouraged to develop impactful proposals that align with NATO’s strategic priorities and support sustainable transatlantic partnerships.

Eligible Areas of Support

Projects supported under the grants program may include:

  • Defense and security policy research
  • NATO awareness and educational initiatives
  • Strategic dialogue forums and workshops
  • Industrial cooperation and defense innovation projects
  • Capacity-building activities
  • Public diplomacy and outreach campaigns
  • Academic conferences and policy engagement programs
  • Networking initiatives among experts and institutions
  • Collaborative research on collective defense

Programs that demonstrate measurable impact, international collaboration, and alignment with NATO priorities are especially encouraged.

Application Guidelines

Applicants must carefully follow all application requirements and submission instructions.

Important Guidelines

  • All application forms must be completed in English.
  • Budgets and financial figures must be converted into U.S. dollars (USD).
  • Programs involving travel to or from the United States must comply with the Fly America Act.
  • Successful grantees will work closely with assigned Grants Officers and Grants Officer Representatives to ensure proper implementation and allowable program costs.

Applicants are advised to prepare detailed proposals that clearly explain:

  • Project goals
  • Expected outcomes
  • Target audience
  • Budget breakdown
  • Timeline
  • Strategic relevance to NATO priorities

Where to Apply

All available grant opportunities offered by the U.S. Mission to NATO are posted online through Grants.gov to ensure transparency and open competition.

Applicants should regularly monitor the official portal for updates, application requirements, and deadlines.

Explore grants and apply here

Official Residence: Truman Hall

The official residence associated with the U.S. Mission to NATO is Truman Hall, a historic Flemish country estate located in Belgium.

Built in 1963 for Belgian chocolatier Jean Michiels of Côte d’Or, the estate later became the residence of the U.S. Ambassador to NATO. Named after President Harry S. Truman, one of NATO’s founders, the residence symbolizes the enduring partnership between the United States and Europe.

Features of Truman Hall

The estate includes:

  • Expansive formal gardens
  • Landscaped meadows and valleys
  • English gardens and herb gardens
  • A pavilion surrounded by flowering plants
  • Historic architectural features
  • Belgian black marble interiors
  • A library with 18th-century wood paneling

The landscape design was created by renowned architect René Pechère, who transformed agricultural land into a scenic diplomatic residence welcoming visitors from NATO member and partner countries.

A special commemorative garden featuring cobbled circles representing NATO countries was added in 1984.

Truman Hall remains an important symbol of diplomacy, cooperation, and transatlantic partnership.

Art in Embassies Program

The U.S. Department of State’s Art in Embassies (AIE) program also plays a significant role at the U.S. Mission to NATO.

Established in 1963, the program promotes cultural diplomacy by curating exhibitions and artistic installations in U.S. embassies, consulates, and diplomatic residences worldwide.

The NATO permanent collection honors:

  • The United States
  • The U.S. Armed Forces
  • Shared democratic values
  • International cultural exchange

Through art and cultural engagement, the program strengthens mutual understanding among nations and highlights the diversity of American and international artistic expression.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The U.S. Mission to NATO grants initiative offers an important platform for organizations and institutions seeking to contribute to international security, defense cooperation, strategic policy development, and industrial resilience.

The program provides opportunities to:

  • Expand transatlantic partnerships
  • Influence defense and security dialogue
  • Support NATO priorities
  • Build institutional capacity
  • Engage with international policymakers and experts
  • Contribute to global peace and collective defense efforts

For institutions focused on international relations, security studies, defense policy, innovation, diplomacy, and public engagement, this funding opportunity can serve as a major catalyst for impactful projects and international collaboration.

Deadline

No specific application deadline was stated in the provided information. Applicants are encouraged to check the official Grants.gov portal regularly for updated submission timelines and funding notices.

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more grant opportunities here

The post U.S. Mission to NATO Grants Program 2025: Funding Opportunities to Advance Transatlantic Defense, Security, and Industrial Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
